zh.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro是Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández和Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra的兒子，於1220年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安。他和Teresa Alfonso de León至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1275年9月8日在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞艾其亞享年55歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro是Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández和Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra的儿子，于1220年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安。他和Teresa Alfonso de León至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1275年9月8日在西班牙安達盧希亞澤維利亞艾其亞享年55岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞。

nameMeanings.name: Gonzalez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Jorge, Pedro, Francisco, Miguel, Ramon, Raul, Mario.
Spanish (González): patronymic from the personal name Gonzalo . Compare De Gonzalez , Gonsales , Gunsalus , and Portuguese Goncalves .

variants.name: Gonzelez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonsalez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Ruben, Ignacio, Manuel, Alfredo, Elena, Felipe, Guadalupe, Gustavo, Jesus, Jorge.
Hispanic (Gonsález): altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gongalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzolas
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonsales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Ruben, Arcadio, Artemio, Erasmo, Ernesto, Eusebio, Juan, Lupe, Manuel, Miguel.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzalo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Miguel, Pedro, Albino, Amador, Angelina, Arana, Arnaldo, Blanco, Carlos, Casimiro, Castro, Diego.
Spanish: from the personal name Gonzalo, of Visigothic origin, formed with the element gunth ‘battle’.

variants.name: De Gonzalez
meanings: Spanish (De González): patronymic from González (see Gonzalez ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gozalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Ramon, Ruben, Pedro, Raul, Guadalupe, Lupe.
Spanish: altered form of González (see Gonzalez ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Pedro, Jesus, Roberto, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Lara de los Infantes in Burgos province. Compare   De Lara  .

variants.name: De Lara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Juan, Jose, Luis, Mauricio, Pedro, Alfonso, Ana, Jaime, Javier, Jorge, Merlinda.
Spanish: variant, with the preposition de ‘from’, of Lara .

nameMeanings.name: El
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written ឯម, unexplained.
Perhaps also an altered form of German   Ehm  .

variants.name: Ehm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: variant of Öhm (see Ohm ).

nameMeanings.name: Bueno
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Raul, Francisco, Ramon, Ana, Miguel, Javier, Jorge.
Spanish: generally an approving (or ironic) nickname, from Spanishbueno ‘good’.

nameMeanings.name: Diaz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Rafael, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto.
Spanish (Díaz): patronymic from the medieval personal name Didacus (see Diego ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Diazdeleon
meanings: Spanish: combination of the surname Díaz (see Diaz ) and the name León (see Leon ) with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Diego
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Francisco, Juan, Fernando, Jose, Miguel, Mario, Maximo, Salvador, Aida, Ana, Domingo.

Spanish: from the very common male personal name Diego, which in the Spanish-speaking world is usually taken to be a shortened form of Santiago . However, this may be only a folk etymology. The personal name is found in the Middle Ages in the Latin forms Didacus and Didagus, probably a derivative of Greek didachē ‘doctrine, teaching’. However, in view of the fact that this personal name is unknown outside the Iberian Peninsula, it may actually have a pre-Roman origin.

variants.name: Diez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Fernando, Pedro, Rafael, Roberto, Angel, Eduardo, Mario, Ricardo.
Spanish (Díez): variant of Díaz (see Diaz ).
German: variant of   Dietz  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Haro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Juan, Luis, Salvador, Manuel, Carlos, Jorge, Miguel, Alberto, Francisco, Jaime.
Spanish: habitational name from a place in the province of La Rioja, so called from a North Castilian form of Spanish faro ‘beacon’. Compare De Haro .
In some cases possibly also French: metonymic occupational name for a peddler, from an Old French cry used to hail someone or to attract attention.

variants.name: De Haro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Rafael, Arturo, Jaime, Jesus, Ubaldo, Alfredo, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Gustavo, Juan.
Spanish: habitational name, formed with the preposition de ‘from’, for someone from a place called Haro (see Haro ).

variants.name: Haros
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): probably the plural form of Spanish   Haro  .
Greek: variant of   Charos  .

nameMeanings.name: Nuño
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Úna , assimilated to the name of the Roman goddess Juno, consort of Jupiter.

en.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro was born in 1220, in Castilla y León, Spain as the son of Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández and Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra. He had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Teresa Alfonso de León. He died on 8 September 1275, in Écija, Sevilla, Andalusia, Spain, at the age of 55, and was buried in Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro est né en 1220, à Castilla y León, Espagne, fils de Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández et Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Teresa Alfonso de León. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1275, à Écija, Sevilla, Andalucía, Espagne, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro wurde 1220 in Castilla y León, Spanien, als Sohn von Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández und Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra geboren. Er hatte mit Teresa Alfonso de León mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 8. September 1275 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Écija, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spanien und wurde in Palencia, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Teresa Alfonso de León. Morì il 8 settembre 1275, nel Écija, Sevilla, Andalucía, Spagna, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Palencia, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ko.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro(은)는 1220년 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o에서 Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández(와)과 Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Teresa Alfonso de León(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1275년 9월 8일 스페인 앤다루ci아 세비라 e시자에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 파렌샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro nasceu em 1220, em Castilla y León, Espanha filho de Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández e Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas com Teresa Alfonso de León. Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1275, em Écija, Sevilla, Andaluzia, Espanha, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.Palencia, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spa: пеиленся, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro родился в 1220 г. в местности кастилла и леoн, Испания. Его родителей звали Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández и Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Teresa Alfonso de León. Он умер 8 сентября 1275 г. в местности eсиджа, севилла, андалукiа, Испания в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro nació en 1220, en Castilla y León, España como hijo de Gonzalo Pérez  Fernández y Doña María Díaz de Haro Perez de Azagra. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Teresa Alfonso de León. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1275, en Écija, Sevilla, Andalucía, España, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Palencia, Castilla y León, España.
